untranslatable_reason: Bull put spread is an options strategy involving specific strike prices (lower strike 50, higher strike 60) and credit amounts (5 points). The backtest engine operates on price/technical indicators only and cannot model multi-leg options positions, strike selection, or credit spread mechanics. The signal requires options chain data and portfolio-level position management that exceed the scope of price-based technical analysis rules.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_141 describes a credit spread options strategy. While the regime condition (TRENDING) can be expressed, the core strategy—buying/selling specific puts at defined strikes and profiting from time decay and directional bias—requires options pricing models and position management outside this backtest engine's capabilities. To adapt: consider using a proxy rule like 'buy when price closes above a moving average within a trending regime' but this would not accurately represent the spread's risk/reward profile.
